symptoms.symptom_name: Exertional heavy retrosternal chest pressure with a reproducible activity threshold

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reproducible effort-induced retrosternal pressure is the defining feature of angina.
Exertional cardiac pain shares the ischaemic substrate; new onset keeps ACS in play.

symptoms.symptom_name: Radiation of pain to the ulnar aspect of the left arm

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Left-arm radiation is a classic anginal feature raising the likelihood of ischaemic cardiac pain.
Radiating heavy pressure is atypical for a localised chest-wall source.

symptoms.symptom_name: Complete resolution of pain within 5-10 minutes of rest

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rapid rest-relief reflects restoration of the supply/demand balance — hallmark of stable, not unstable, angina.
Prompt rest-relief argues against ongoing/unstable ischaemia, but does not exclude an ACS substrate on a new presentation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of chest pain at rest and no nocturnal/waking pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rest and nocturnal pain define unstable angina; their absence supports current stability.
Purely effort-limited symptoms with no rest component characterise stable angina.

symptoms.symptom_name: Exertional breathlessness coinciding with and resolving with the chest pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Exertional dyspnoea is a common anginal equivalent reflecting transient ischaemic LV dysfunction.
Cold-induced peripheral vasoconstriction raises afterload and myocardial oxygen demand, lowering the angina threshold.

symptoms.symptom_name: New onset within 4 weeks (no prior similar episodes)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: New-onset angina overlaps the unstable spectrum until objectively characterised — mandates ECG/troponin work-up.

symptoms.symptom_name: Cardiac risk factors: hypertension and hypercholesterolaemia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Established coronary risk factors raise the pre-test probability of obstructive coronary disease.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal resting vital signs (BP 135/85, HR 76, RR 16, SpO2 99%, afebrile)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal resting observations are expected in stable angina and do not exclude coronary disease; here they were clinician-measured in person (sealed).

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal cardiorespiratory examination (dual heart sounds, no murmur/gallop, no raised JVP, clear lungs, non-reproducible on palpation)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pain not reproduced on palpation argues against a chest-wall source; requires hands-on examination, unavailable via telehealth.
Clear lungs, no gallop and no raised JVP argue against decompensated failure.

symptoms.symptom_name: Transient ST depression on exercise (anticipated on formal stress ECG)

investigation_details.expected_result: Transient horizontal/downsloping ST depression at the ischaemic threshold
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The stable pattern remains unconfirmed and inducible ischaemia undocumented.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reversible exertional ST depression documents inducible myocardial ischaemia — objective confirmation not obtainable via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Reproducible exertional retrosternal pressure + left-arm radiation + prompt rest-relief together give a high positive likelihood for angina, but do not by themselves exclude ACS on a new presentation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line objective test to look for ischaemic changes and help exclude ACS in new cardiac-type chest pain.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum troponin (if any diagnostic doubt or interval symptoms)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Excludes myocardial injury when the history cannot confidently separate stable angina from an ACS.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Exercise ECG stress test or CT coronary angiography (fast-track outpatient)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms inducible ischaemia / coronary anatomy and stratifies risk once ACS has been excluded.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Fasting lipids, HbA1c, renal function and cardiovascular risk profiling
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Optimises secondary prevention and modifiable risk factors.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine chest X-ray as the primary work-up for this presentation
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: A chest X-ray does not diagnose or exclude coronary ischaemia and should not delay ECG-based assessment.
